_comment: RAT notifications/v1 conformance vectors — golden data for the experience-axis `notifications` plugin. A `kind: notifications` plugin (slack, email, webhook, PagerDuty) is a DELIVERY SINK: given a (severity, title, body, target, attributes) it delivers one message and reports (delivered, message_id). WHAT to notify on is the operator's subscription config (overview.md reconciliation: subscriptions = [event, action]), NOT this plugin — so these vectors only exercise the delivery contract via NotificationsService.Send. The reference (Python: examples/notifications/inmemory-py) is an in-memory sink that CAPTURES notifications instead of delivering them anywhere, so the harness can assert each send reports delivered + a non-empty message_id and the sink captured exactly the delivered cases. The `severity` strings map to the proto Severity enum by name (SEVERITY_<NAME>). Notifications is NOT tenant-scoped, so unlike storage there is no caller-tenant in these vectors. Emitters MUST NOT include secrets in title/body (I13).
